message:
perf(fsqlite-types): swap std::str::from_utf8 for simdutf8 on TEXT hot paths

Real SQL workloads are overwhelmingly ASCII- or majority-ASCII, so
UTF-8 validation dominates small-value decoding costs that should be
nearly free. simdutf8 is a drop-in replacement for std::str::from_utf8
that uses runtime-dispatched SIMD (SSE4.2/AVX2/NEON) and is typically
3-10x faster on ASCII inputs while matching std on pathological non-
ASCII cases. It keeps the same semantics (returns Utf8Error on
invalid input) and integrates under forbid(unsafe_code) since it
exposes the validator as a safe function — important because
SmallText currently can't reach for from_utf8_unchecked.

Call sites updated:
  - record.rs `decode_value_into`: both the TEXT slot-reuse path and
    the fresh-allocation path. These run once per TEXT column per
    decoded row in the payload-record reader.
  - value.rs `SmallText::as_str`: hot inline accessor. The inline
    buffer is valid UTF-8 by construction, but forbid(unsafe_code)
    forces us to pay for validation on every read, so making that
    validation SIMD-accelerated is the biggest single win.
  - value.rs `SmallText::into_arc`: consumed-by-Arc conversion with
    the same forbid(unsafe_code) constraint as as_str.

Cargo wiring:
  - Workspace root declares `simdutf8 = "0.1"` alongside the other
    string/encoding deps.
  - fsqlite-types picks it up as a workspace dependency next to memchr
    and xxhash-rust, its other SIMD-leaning utilities.
  - Cargo.lock updated for the new dep graph.

Rationale comments added at each call site explaining why we're not
using std::str::from_utf8 (or from_utf8_unchecked) so future readers
don't "optimize" the simdutf8 call back out.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
